issues.title: Attributes from RFC7317
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body: Some of the hardware and software attributes defined in Section 3.1 of [RFC7317] can be borrowed, such as platform-os version and machine.

comments.author: italobusi
comments.body:
WG adoption comment from Bo:

> 1. Section 2.2. Network Element: Adds a reference of RFC 7317 to indicate that the NE software version is the platform software version.

See: https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/inventory-yang/MT7vspJ4AmI8I0YeLgPJCOrmjMk/

issues.title: Child and parent component navigation
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body: Need to discuss if to use parent-component-ref as in the original CCAMP WG draft or to define a parent attribute as in RFC8348

comments.author: italobusi
comments.body:
WG adoption comment from Bo:

> 2. Section 2.4.1. New Parent Identifiers' Reference: The YANG model has no definition on this. Suggest to define the component relationship rather than parent relationship, and also adds the child component relationship, e.g., define the child component as a list.

See: https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/inventory-yang/MT7vspJ4AmI8I0YeLgPJCOrmjMk/

comments.author: italobusi
comments.body: I think we can keep the text but move it to an appendix of the document

issues.title: Example of YANG code for the grouping
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body: Add an example of YANG code for the grouping (Component-Specific Info Design section)

issues.title: Software patch
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body: Whether the software patch is in the base inventory model is to be discussed.

issues.title: Write operations
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body:
Need to further discuss whether the base network inventory YANG model should support write operations (RW) or not (RO)

See:

- https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/inventory-yang/Z6reCPdR3UGbHFl152AhB1_QIvw/
- https://github.com/ietf-ccamp-wg/ietf-network-inventory/issues/95
- https://github.com/ietf-ccamp-wg/ietf-network-inventory/issues/82
- https://github.com/ietf-ccamp-wg/ietf-network-inventory/issues/54

comments.author: italobusi
comments.body:
Please note that changing from RO->RW can be BC while changing RW->RO is NBC

See [section 11 of RFC7950](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7950#section-11):

```
   o  A node that represented state data may be changed to represent
      configuration, provided it is not mandatory (Section 3).
```

issues.title: WG adoption comments from Med
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body:
> Some quick comments:
> 
>   *   Please soften the various citations to ACTN. I would simply remove them.
>   *   Consider adding a dedicated section to describe the provisions in the model for future augmentations.
>   *   Consider if some data nodes that are defined in other models can be added directly to the base model, e.g., the following ones from draft-li
> 
>        +--ro required-components*          -> ../uuid
>        +--ro dependent-components*         -> ../uuid

See: https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/inventory-yang/dVKTwV_pSSnG9-vsxH0THDQcuLI/

issues.title: WG adoption comments from Qiufang
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body:
> As discussed in the design team call which happened last week, I would like the authors to consider adding a requirement or use cases section to illustrate what we would like to cover in this draft. We had some discussion about what should and what should not be in the scope of this draft both in the IETF meeting and in the weekly calls. I think it would be helpful if the requirements or use cases could be discussed and agreed upon.

See: https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/inventory-yang/FrD6aIOfr8IKCM0-6uoi188gOgg/

initial proposal for base model draft as discussed during the call 10-18-23

reviews.author: QiufangMa
reviews.body: I think you might need to rename the file to draft-y3bp-ivy-network-inventory-yang*, otherwise we won't be able to see it in IVY doc list.
